Phase-37.NAME: Live MTProto Shared-443 Runtime Fix
Phase-37.Goal: Fix the clean-VPS production defect where KrotPN issued MTProto proxy links but Telegram marked them unavailable because no live MTProto fake-TLS edge owned public TCP 443.

OperatorProblem.item-1: Clean deploy, email verification, login, and dashboard issuance worked.
OperatorProblem.item-2: The copied Telegram Desktop proxy link was unavailable.
OperatorProblem.item-3: Live inspection showed nginx owned public TCP 443 and no MTProto runtime/policy process was running.

RootCause.item-1: Phase-30/34 issued assignments and simulated runtime policy apply locally, but production compose did not start KPprotoN/mtproto_proxy.
RootCause.item-2: Public TCP 443 was served by normal HTTPS nginx, so Telegram fake-TLS traffic could not reach MTProto runtime classification.
RootCause.item-3: The backend returned an active proxy payload before proving the issued SNI was loaded into a real edge runtime.

NonGoals.item-1: No rotation of MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX or MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT.
NonGoals.item-2: No change to already-issued assignment rows except replaying their SNI policies into the live runtime.
NonGoals.item-3: No replacement of the operator-approved wildcard TLS installer flow.
NonGoals.item-4: No frontend redesign in this hotfix phase.

DataFlow.flow-1: deploy/deploy-on-server.sh creates MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN and writes the localhost policy URL and shared-443 fallback env values.
DataFlow.flow-2: docker-compose starts mtproto-edge in host network mode with PROXY_PORT=443, trusted wildcard certs, and a domain-fronting fallback to 127.0.0.1:8443.
DataFlow.flow-3: nginx serves normal HTTPS fallback on 8443 while HTTP port 80 still performs the canonical HTTPS redirect.
DataFlow.flow-4: backend/app/mtproto/runtime_bridge.py uses HTTPMTProtoPolicyAdapter when MTPROTO_RUNTIME_POLICY_URL and MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN are configured.
DataFlow.flow-5: backend/app/mtproto/provisioning.py applies the issued SNI through the bridge before returning an active Telegram proxy payload.
DataFlow.flow-6: deploy/deploy-on-server.sh routes Telegram MTProto upstream ranges through RU awg-client to DE while leaving the host default route unchanged.
DataFlow.flow-7: mtproto-runtime serves bounded local bootstrap secret/config responses with static fallback if core.telegram.org is slow or blocked.
DataFlow.flow-8: Startup/scheduler replay loads existing active assignments into the live policy table after container restart or hotfix deploy.

Steps.step-1: Import the required KPprotoN runtime surface as mtproto-runtime without generated build artifacts.
Steps.step-2: Add a token-protected local Erlang policy handler for apply, revoke, and health operations.
Steps.step-3: Add backend HTTPMTProtoPolicyAdapter and select it from Settings when the runtime URL/token are present.
Steps.step-4: Change provisioning to require live policy apply before returning an active proxy payload.
Steps.step-5: Move public TCP 443 ownership from nginx to mtproto-edge and move nginx HTTPS fallback to 8443.
Steps.step-6: Apply the hotfix to the RU server, route Telegram upstream ranges through awg-client, ensure mtproto_proxy binds public 443 after bounded bootstrap fallback, replay existing active assignment SNI, and collect redacted HTTPS/policy health evidence before the operator retests Telegram Desktop.

Verification.command-2: rm -rf /tmp/krotpn-mtproto-runtime-check && rsync -a --exclude='_build' --exclude='rebar3.crashdump' mtproto-runtime/ /tmp/krotpn-mtproto-runtime-check/ && cd /tmp/krotpn-mtproto-runtime-check && rebar3 as prod release
Verification.command-3: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py -v
Verification.command-5: DB_PASSWORD=phase37-compose-check SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length POSTGRES_PASSWORD=phase37-postgres REDIS_PASSWORD=phase37-redis JWT_SECRET_KEY=phase37-jwt DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX=00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT=ffeeddccbbaa99887766554433221100 MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN=phase37-runtime-token-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz MTPROTO_RUNTIME_POLICY_URL=http://127.0.0.1:18080/krotpn/mtproto/policy docker compose config
Verification.command-6: node scripts/phase32-edge-contract-smoke.mjs && node scripts/phase34-mtproto-stabilization-smoke.mjs && node scripts/phase35-installer-wildcard-tls-smoke.mjs && node scripts/phase37-mtproto-runtime-smoke.mjs
Verification.command-7: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-044.xml docs/modules/M-046.xml docs/modules/M-048.xml docs/modules/M-049.xml docs/plans/Phase-37.xml docs/verification/V-M-049.xml && mygrace lint
Verification.command-8: operator-live: on RU, verify mtproto-edge owns TCP 443, nginx owns fallback 8443, policy health is healthy, public HTTPS still responds, and the operator-retained Telegram Desktop proxy becomes available.

VerificationMatrix.gate-1: docker-compose has mtproto-edge host-network service and nginx does not bind public 443.
VerificationMatrix.gate-2: deploy generates MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN, policy URL, policy port, shared-443 flag, and HTTPS fallback port without printing secrets.
VerificationMatrix.gate-3: backend HTTP adapter sends apply/revoke/health to the local policy API with the token header and redacts token/secret material.
VerificationMatrix.gate-4: provisioning does not present an active proxy as usable until runtime policy apply succeeds.
VerificationMatrix.gate-5: After hotfix deploy, Telegram upstream ranges route through awg-client, public HTTPS remains available through fallback, mtproto_proxy binds public 443, and issued MTProto SNI is loaded in live policy.
VerificationMatrix.gate-6: Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available using the existing dashboard link.

TelemetryRequirements.redaction-1: Never log MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, derived secrets, full tg:// links, certificate private keys, JWTs, or verification tokens.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Fresh deploy starts mtproto-edge and backend without blank-secret or missing-token failures.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Public TCP 443 is not bound by nginx; nginx HTTPS fallback is bound on 8443.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Policy health endpoint is reachable only with the deploy-generated token from localhost.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: MTProto bootstrap succeeds through bounded local core fetch or static fallback and public TCP 443 is bound by mtproto-edge.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-5: Existing active assignment SNI is applied to the live policy table without rotating proxy credentials.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-6: https://krotpn.xyz remains reachable after shared-443 change.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-7: Telegram Desktop proxy availability is restored after operator retest.

Risks.risk-1: Host-network mtproto-edge can break HTTPS if fallback target or wildcard cert mount is wrong.
Risks.risk-2: Changing public 443 ownership requires careful live rollout and rollback evidence.
Risks.risk-3: Telegram availability is ultimately client/network dependent; local evidence can prove edge policy and ports but not replace operator Telegram client proof.

ExecutionEvidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-18: Local diagnosis on RU confirmed backend/frontend/nginx were healthy, but no MTProto process existed and nginx owned public TCP 443.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-18: Local implementation added mtproto-runtime, policy API, backend HTTP adapter, provisioning live-apply, compose service, nginx 8443 fallback, and deploy env wiring.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Live RU first run showed policy API healthy but mtproto_proxy bootstrap timed out against Telegram core; Phase-37 adds bounded static bootstrap fallback and requires TCP 443 bind evidence.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: After Telegram routes via awg-client, TCP reachability improved but mtproto_proxy downstream handshakes still closed; nginx was temporarily restored on public 443 to keep KrotPN web available.
